activity.title: United States et al. v. RealPage, Inc.et al.; Response of the United States to Public Comments
activity.raw_text: United States et al. v. RealPage, Inc.et al.; Response of the United States to Public Comments

enrichment.summary: The DOJ Antitrust Division filed a response to public comments in the United States et al. v. RealPage, Inc. et al. case, published in the Federal Register. RealPage is a software company whose algorithmic pricing tools for rental housing have been the subject of antitrust scrutiny. This filing represents a procedural step in the consent decree or settlement process under the Tunney Act, where the government responds to public input before a court enters a final judgment.

source_entity.name: DOJ Antitrust Division
source_entity.description: Investigates AI-market concentration with FTC under a jurisdictional split (DOJ took Nvidia, Microsoft; FTC took OpenAI, Anthropic, Google). Live merger reviews shape compute-market structure; Google search remedies trial sets precedent for AI-product distribution mandates.
